message.content:
```markdown
## Known Vulnerabilities

| CVE / GHSA | Summary | Severity | Affected Versions | Patched In | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CVE-2019-1010266 / GHSA-x5rq-j2xg-h7qm | ReDoS in certain methods | Moderate | <4.17.11 | 4.17.11 | |
| CVE-2019-10744 / GHSA-jf85-cpcp-j695 | Prototype pollution via `_.defaultsDeep` | Critical | <4.17.12 | 4.17.12 | |
| CVE-2020-8203 / GHSA-p6mc-m468-83gw | Prototype pollution via `_.zipObjectDeep` | High | <4.17.19 | 4.17.19 | |
| CVE-2020-28500 / GHSA-29mw-wpgm-hmr9 | ReDoS in `_.toNumber`, `_.trim`, `_.trimEnd` | Moderate | <4.17.21 | 4.17.21 | |
| CVE-2021-23337 / GHSA-35jh-r3h4-6jhm | Command injection via `_.template` `variable` option | High | <4.17.21 | 4.17.21 | Fix was incomplete; see CVE-2026-4800 |
| CVE-2025-13465 / GHSA-xxjr-mmjv-4gpg | Prototype pollution via `_.unset` / `_.omit` | High | 4.0.0–4.17.22 | 4.17.23 | Fix was incomplete; see CVE-2026-2950 |
| CVE-2026-2950 / GHSA-f23m-r3pf-42rh | Bypass of CVE-2025-13465 fix via array-wrapped path segments in `_.unset` / `_.omit` | High | <4.18.0 | 4.18.0 | Bypass of prior patch: string-key guard did not cover array path segments |
| CVE-2026-4800 / GHSA-r5fr-rjxr-66jc | Code injection via `_.template` `options.imports` key names | High | <4.18.0 | 4.18.0 | Bypass of CVE-2021-23337 fix: `variable` was validated but `imports` key names were not; fix also switched `assignInWith` → `assignWith` to block inherited polluted keys |

## Security Posture

- **Incomplete-fix chains are a recurring pattern.** Two separate vulnerability families required multi-stage fixes spanning years: `_.template` injection (2021 → 2026) and `_.unset`/`_.omit` prototype pollution (2025 → 2026). Consumers on intermediate patch versions (e.g., 4.17.21 or 4.17.23) remain exposed to the respective bypasses. **Upgrading to ≥4.18.0 is strongly recommended.**
- **Prototype pollution has been the dominant vulnerability class**, accounting for at least four distinct CVEs across different lodash surface areas (`defaultsDeep`, `zipObjectDeep`, `unset`/`omit`, `merge`-adjacent patterns). This reflects inherent risk in lodash's deep-path manipulation utilities operating on arbitrary user-controlled input.
- **Still extremely widely depended upon.** npm shows ~133M weekly downloads and latest published version is 4.18.1. The massive install base means even moderate-severity issues carry outsized ecosystem impact.
- **Maintenance cadence has improved recently.** After a long period of minimal releases (4.17.21 was the last release for several years), 4.17.23 and 4.18.0 were published to address the 2025–2026 advisories, indicating renewed maintainer responsiveness.
- **Key commits for auditability:** Prototype-pollution fix in `baseUnset`: `edadd452146f`; template injection fix (variable option): `3469357cff39`. Reviewing these commits is useful for understanding scope of the guards and residual attack surface.
- **Template-related injection risk remains elevated for applications passing user-controlled data to `_.template` options.** Even with the 4.18.0 fix, `_.template` compiles strings into `Function()` calls; any use with untrusted input should be treated as inherently dangerous regardless of lodash version.

## Editorial Note

The page should explicitly call out the two **incomplete-fix chains**:

1. **`_.template` injection chain:** CVE-2021-23337 (patched 4.17.21) → CVE-2026-4800 (patched 4.18.0). The original fix validated the `variable` option but did not sanitize `options.imports` key names, and used `assignInWith` which allowed inherited
